use std::io::Read;
use std::net::TcpStream;

const MAX_REQUEST_SIZE: usize = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB

// GOOD: size-limited read before parsing
fn handle_connection(mut stream: TcpStream) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let mut buf = vec![0u8; MAX_REQUEST_SIZE];
    let bytes_read = stream.read(&mut buf)?;
    buf.truncate(bytes_read);

    let request: Request = serde_json::from_slice(&buf)?;
    process_request(request);
    Ok(())
}

fn load_and_parse(path: &str) -> Result<Data, Error> {
    let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path)?;
    if metadata.len() > MAX_REQUEST_SIZE as u64 {
        return Err(Error::FileTooLarge);
    }
    let content = std::fs::read(path)?;
    let data: Data = serde_json::from_slice(&content)?;
    Ok(data)
}

fn read_request(body: &mut dyn Read) -> Result<Request, Error> {
    let mut buf = Vec::with_capacity(MAX_REQUEST_SIZE);
    let mut limited = body.take(MAX_REQUEST_SIZE as u64);
    limited.read_to_end(&mut buf)?;
    let request: Request = serde_json::from_slice(&buf)?;
    Ok(request)
}
